_comment: Persona page queue. Each entry produces ONE JSON at content/data/personas/<slug>.json via coveredusa-persona-writer.

personas.notes: Schedule C filers, sole proprietors, freelancers. Self-employed health insurance tax deduction (Form 7206, 100% above-the-line). HSA-qualified HDHP attractive. MAGI projection for ACA subsidies. Different from gig workers — these are typically traditional freelancers (designers, consultants, lawyers) with higher income.

Options: parent's plan (under 26), school student health plan (often cheap, $1K-$3K/year, sometimes mandatory), Medicaid if low income, marketplace SEP if turning 26. Common gotcha: out-of-state coverage limits on parent's HMO.

Retirement before 65 = lose employer coverage but not eligible for Medicare. Bridge years 60-65 critical. Options: ACA marketplace, COBRA, spouse's plan. Lower retirement income = larger subsidies. 401k withdrawals count as MAGI.

SHOP marketplace for businesses with <50 employees. ICHRA (Individual Coverage HRA) — newer option, employer reimburses employee for individual marketplace plan. Self-employed deduction. QSEHRA for very small employers. Different from gig/freelance because there are EMPLOYEES involved.

Income under 138% FPL ($22,025 hh-1). Medicaid in expansion states. The 10 non-expansion state coverage gap. FQHCs (Federally Qualified Health Centers) for free/sliding-scale care. CHIP for kids. Critical: many qualify but don't know they do.

personas._defer_reason: VA priority groups (1-8), PACT Act expansion, CHAMPVA (for dependents not vets), TRICARE plan variants don't fit the optionsOverview/optionDetails table model. Build dedicated veteran schema before generating.

personas.notes: Typically on spouse's employer plan. If divorced/widowed: marketplace with no income (use household MAGI), Medicaid if low. CHIP for children. Tax filing status (joint vs single) matters for marketplace subsidies.

Most employers don't have to offer coverage for under-30-hour employees (ACA threshold). Options: marketplace with subsidies, Medicaid if low income, spouse plan. Some employers offer 'mini-med' plans which are usually traps.
Usually 1099 or off-the-books. ACA marketplace primary option. Medicaid if low income (most qualify). NDWA advocacy resources. Cash-pay challenges with tax documentation for marketplace MAGI.

personas._defer_reason: Legal accuracy risk: lawful presence vs PRUCOL vs undocumented vs DACA categories each have distinct rules. Public charge concerns. State-funded Medicaid for undocumented varies (CA paused 19-49 enrollment in 2026). Needs legal-services review (NILC) before generation. FQHC + emergency Medicaid universal — those are safe to mention.
